transcript.text:
Top 3 Tteokboki in Seoul
[A street-style tteokboki shop that holds its name in Seoul]
[You can't forget about soondae and fried mandu] (Pork blood sausage and dumplings)
You can't eat this by yourself
-I'm a big eater -No, still
Are you Haetnim?
[I'm not Haetnim, but I'm just as good as her at eating]
[I agree...]
Hello everyone
I'm here at Munjeong-dong today
1 of top 3 tteokboki spot in Seoul is located here
It's called Golmok Tteokboki
They're known for their soup-style tteokboki
And I've been wanting to try it
[Golmok Tteokboki] [Seoul, Songpa-gu]
Hello~

They have a self-serve system after ordering
[Utensils]
4 orders of tteokboki,
1 order of mandu,
1 order of soondae
Is someone else coming?
No, it's all for myself
[Tteokboki, a dish that everybody loves] (Spicy rice cake)
[Tteokboki's bestfriend: Soondae] (Pork blood sausage]
[Can't forget about mandu] (Fried dumplings)
[Order up!]
You can't eat all of that by yourself
I'm a big eater
No, still
Are you Haetnim?
[I better work harder...]

That's heavy
Bon appetit
This is the famous soup tteokboki
Some of the soup first
That's yummy
It's like eating stew
Tteokboki stew?
Perfect for a hangover
Some rice cake now
Flour rice cake
A chewy one at that
Now with some fishcake
Stab it with the stick to make a skewer
The flavors are very familiar
Some spiciness and sweetness
Savory

A flavor that brings back memories
Scoop it with a spoon like this
This is why they didn't have chopsticks
To eat it with soup
The outside is so soft to the point it's almost falling apart
And the inside is chewy
The seasoning is soaked in well
Get it with some soup like this
Some soondae now
The glossiness
That's tasty
Brings back great memories
Some mandu too With some soup
I like this part of it
I went up 3 octaves That's good
Soondae

Soak it in
Get the soup on it
Mandu too
I don't like mandu too much if the flour is overwhelming, but this isn't like that
And it has chewy insides
Dip the soondae a little
It's all over my pants
Soondae and rice cake
Stir it a little
It didn't feel too special at first
But it gets very addicting as you eat
It gets better and better
The rice cake is very chewy and soft! Goes down with ease
Soondae on top
I'm not getting sick of the same flavors
It's starting to get a little spicy But it's clean

Not too strong
I saw on the blog that a lot of people come here after a night of drinking
I can now see why people do
What are some other Top 3 tteokboki places?
"Shintoboolee" from Achasan for sure
This place for sure too
The last one? I'm not sure, I guess it depends on your preference
Lovely
I'm going to need to order more mandu because it's so good
I don't really add more food during my mukbang
But it's really good There's not much to it
But how?
What is it?
Can I get 1 order of mandu?
Thank you
Did it get too soggy?

Still delicious
Nothing too special, but it's very addicting
Even the mandu as well
Nothing special, but really good
Dip the half like this
And scoop some inside
It tastes better and better
Dip in the soup
Feels like I'm curing a hangover
Did I drink last night? I don't remember drinking
Soondae tastes good too
Everything is very standard
But it's really delicious
I can see both young and old coming in and out of the shop
The elders will enjoy these too
Yummy yummy It keeps getting better

Skewer
Rice cake, mandu, soondae, fishcake
How am I supposed to eat this? Seems impossible
Getting the mandu by itself and without the soup gets you the true flavors and crispiness
If you dip it before you eat it It will stay crispy
It's less spicy than Shin Ramyun noodles
It's all gone already? I ate too fast
Last bite
It was my first time trying the Top 3 tteokboki in Seoul
It felt as if I was here for a hangover I had a great meal
Thanks everyone
Let's drink the soup
[Subtitles by Hyun Choi] [antharoni@gmail.com]
